The Big Wedding (2013)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $21,819,348Details
International Box Office $26,606,623
Worldwide Box Office $48,425,971
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $4,488,645
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,159,866
Total $5,648,511
Further financial details...

Synopsis

To the amusement of their adult children and friends, long divorced couple Don and Ellie Griffin are once again forced to play the happy couple for the sake of their adopted son's wedding after his ultra conservative biological mother unexpectedly decides to fly halfway across the world to attend. With all of the wedding guests looking on, the Griffins are forced to confront their past, present and future - and hopefully avoid killing each other in the process.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$7,591,663 (34.8% of total gross)
Legs:2.87 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:45.1% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$32,500,000 (worldwide box office is 1.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,633 opening theaters/2,633 max. theaters, 3.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $32,098,332

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: April 26th, 2013 (Wide) by Lionsgate
International Releases: April 25th, 2013 (Wide) (Lebanon)
October 31st, 2013 (Wide) (Peru)
November 15th, 2013 (Wide) (Colombia)
June 26th, 2014 (Wide) (Italy)
Video Release: August 13th, 2013 by Lionsgate Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: R for language, sexual content and brief nudity.
(Rating bulletin 2262, 3/6/2013)
Running Time: 95 minutes
Cast, crew, or production detail: Ensemble, Foreign-Language Remake, Remake
Filming location (country): United States
Plot point: Dysfunctional Family, Religious, Romance, Step-Family
Social setting: Adopted Family, Wedding Day
Subgenre: Romantic Comedy
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Romantic Comedy
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Lionsgate, Two Ton Films
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

To the amusement of their adult children and friends, long divorced couple Don and Ellie Griffin are once again forced to play the happy couple for the sake of their adopted son's wedding after his ultra conservative biological mother unexpectedly decides to fly halfway across the world to attend. With all of the wedding guests looking on, the Griffins are forced to confront their past, present and future - and hopefully avoid killing each other in the process.
